title: The Red Fire Dispatch
description: The original red fire message received at the Sandusky telegraph office, logged at 9:14 p.m. in the traffic ledger. The text describes seven persons moving north on the Huron Road, including a man matching Elias Cole's physical description, and names a bounty of four hundred and eighty dollars payable to the Erie County sheriff's office. The critical detail is in the routing header: the message originated not from Cincinnati or Columbus as the sender's notation claims, but from the Sandusky station's own secondary key — the instrument on the side table connected to the Norwalk spur. Someone sent this message from inside this building.
